calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Betty Lew in Propria Persona.

The Court asked about the property being vacant. Ms. Lew explained that her and her siblings decided to keep the house as a second home, which was also the wish of their parents stated in a handwritten note and that they are waiting for the final distribution so that they can use the house. The Court asked about the request to distribute personal property to herself and if everyone received notice of such. Ms. Lew reported that it is fine with her family, that everyone is in agreement and that she had a paralegal prepare the petition and it was inadvertently left out. The Court stated that it would need to have declarations from all interested parties that they are in agreement with the request to distribute the personal property to Betty Lew and that the declarations should be filed within 2 weeks. Ms. Lew asked if a new order was required and was told that the Court should be able to use the order that it has but if a new order is necessary she would be contacted. The accounting petition was taken under submission as of January 11, 2016.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-19-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Betty Lew (Petitioner) in Propria Persona. David Lew in Propria Persona.

The Court expressed its concerns and explained why it is reluctant to appoint Ms. Lew as Administrator without an Attorney. Ms. Lew stated that her Mother's estate is fairly simple, that there is a house which they are not going to sell and will pass/be transferred to all four Children, that she is reading a book called "How to Probate an Estate" and asked to be allowed to proceed on her own. Mr. Lew raised the issue of social security numbers in the will having been filed and asked if there was any way to take them out of the file. The Court explained that a motion to seal the document or redact it needed to be filed. Ms. Lew stated that she will hire an attorney. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order) with the understanding that Ms. Lew will consult with an Attorney and have them available to handle or participate in what ever she does. Bond is not required. The Court ordered Ms. Lew to cure the problem with the social security numbers that have been filed in the public file.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-05-2014 Continued To Nov-26-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
